title: Peña de Bernal
description: Just outside the city, this towering monolith is one of the largest in the world. Ideal for hiking, photography, and panoramic views, Peña de Bernal offers a dramatic natural escape and a chance to connect with the region’s geological wonders.

title: Museo de la Ciudad
description: Housed in a former colonial building, this museum features contemporary art, photography, and cultural exhibitions. It’s a dynamic space that showcases local and international talent in a historic setting.

title: El Cerrito Archaeological Zone
description: Located in nearby Corregidora, this pre-Hispanic site features pyramids, ceremonial platforms, and ancient relics. It’s a quiet, educational stop that reveals Querétaro’s indigenous roots and architectural legacy.
